sections.paragraph: m. with art. הֶחָלָב, const. חֲלֵב (as if from חָלֵב), with suffix חֲלָבִי milk, whilst fresh, differing from חֶמְאָה, so called from fatness, Gen 18:8 49:12 Pro 27:27. For the phrase אֶרֶץ זָבַת חָלָב וּדְבַשׁ, see under the root זוּב . To suck the milk of nations, poet. for to make their wealth one’s own, claim for oneself, Isa 60:16. (Arabic حَاِيب, حَلَبُ id.; whence حَلَبَ to milk; Eth. ሕሊብ፡ milk.)
